description: British racer who took his first win in 2024 and secured the 2025 World Championship after a late-season comeback.
Dutch driver and youngest-ever F1 starter at 17, later becoming a dominant multi-time champion with Red Bull.
Brazilian rookie and back-to-back F3 and F2 champion who became Brazil's first full-time F1 driver in years.
French-Algerian Red Bull junior who graduated through the feeder series and reached Formula 1 in 2025.
French driver known for resilience and racecraft, highlighted by his 2020 Italian Grand Prix victory.
Mexican race winner known for tire management and consistency across a long career.
Italian young talent who rose rapidly through junior categories and joined Mercedes in Formula 1.
Two-time world champion from Spain, renowned for longevity, racecraft, and all-around driving skill.
Monaco driver with elite one-lap pace and multiple Ferrari wins, established as a leading front-runner.
Canadian driver who reached a podium as a rookie and has remained a long-term Aston Martin driver.
Thai-British racer who returned to F1 with Williams and rebuilt his reputation with consistent performances.
Experienced German driver known for versatility and longevity, with extensive time across multiple teams.
New Zealander who impressed in substitute and full-time opportunities, earning seats within the Red Bull system.
French Grand Prix winner who overcame major financial and career setbacks to become a proven Formula 1 driver.
British rookie and Red Bull junior who rose quickly through the junior ladder into Formula 1.
Argentine driver who returned Argentina to the F1 grid after decades and built momentum through strong junior results.
Seven-time world champion and one of F1's most successful drivers, holding records for wins and pole positions.
Spanish race winner known for technical feedback and consistency across several top Formula 1 teams.
British Mercedes driver with strong qualifying speed and racecraft, securing multiple wins in modern F1.
Finnish multiple race winner recognized for consistency and team contribution through different career phases.
Australian junior champion turned F1 race winner who quickly established himself among the leading drivers.
British prospect who impressed on stand-in appearances and progressed into a full-time Formula 1 opportunity.
